stories.original_headline: Ireland’s Old Irish Goat has survived 3,000 years
stories.original_summary: The Old Irish Goat isn’t just part of folklore — it’s genetically linked to goats that lived in Ireland 3,000 years ago. Scientists analyzed ancient remains and discovered that today’s rare breed shares its strongest DNA ties with Late Bronze Age animals. The finding suggests an unbroken Irish linea
stories.fantasy_headline: Ancient Mountain Horns of Emerald Isle Trace Lineage to Age of Bronze
stories.fantasy_story: Wizards on the Emerald Isle have uncovered a fascinating truth about the rare Mountain Horn goats. By studying ancient remains, they found these beasts share an unbroken magical lineage with their kin from the Age of Bronze, three millennia past. This discovery proves the Mountain Horns are truly creatures of old.

stories.original_headline: Qualcomm won’t be announcing Windows gaming handhelds at GDC after all
stories.original_summary: In January, Qualcomm hinted to The Verge that it might finally bring its powerful Arm-based Snapdragon processors to Windows gaming handhelds at the 2026 Game Developers Conference in San Francisco - just in time to challenge Nvidia's own first Arm gaming CPU and Intel's first dedicated handheld gam
stories.fantasy_headline: Clan Qualcomm Delays Unveiling of Enchanted Gaming Talismans at Grand Fair
stories.fantasy_story: The esteemed Clan Qualcomm has announced a delay in presenting their powerful new enchanted gaming talismans. Originally, they planned to unveil these artifacts, powered by formidable dragon-fire cores, at the upcoming Grand Enchantment Fair. This decision means rival Guilds, Nvidia and Intel, will face less immediate competition in the market for such amusement.
